title: Bedding/Linen - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Bedding/Linen 45 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Homelessness Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness AGAPE HOUSE ESSENTIAL NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, blankets, and household goods depending on availability. Shower facilities also available. Donations accepted. 500 West Harper Road Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm, Friday 9am-noon Showers: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-11:30am and 1pm-2:30pm, Friday 9am-11:30am Email (541) 567-8774 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AMITY CHRISTIAN CHURCH LYDIA'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a clothing closet with shoes, clothing, bedding, some toiletries, and small household items. 1305 Goucher Street Amity, OR 97101 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Wednesday 10am-1pm Email (503) 835-2551 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BETHLEHEM HOUSE OF BREAD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping style food pantry focusing on culturally relevant food and options for those with food allergies, sensitivities, and other dietary or lifestyle preferences such as vegan or vegetarian. Caring and Sharing Store provides, when available, clothing (infant-adult sizes), cleaning supplies, kitchenware (plates, bowls, utensils), personal hygiene supplies (toilet paper, shampoo, toothbrushes, female hygiene products), bedding, and laundry supplies. Also provides a seasonal community garden with 24 raised garden beds available to the public. Accepts donations. 9055 SW Locust Street Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Sunday/Thursday 1pm-3pm, Tuesday 4pm-6pm Caring and Sharing Store: Sunday/Thursday 1pm-3pm Community Garden: Season opens Sunday, May 11, 2024: Reservation required Donations: Tuesday-Friday 9am-4pm, Sunday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 639-3181 1 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CANYON CRISIS AND RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, information and referral, support groups, outreach services, counseling, and advocacy. Help with filling out restraining orders and victim compensation papers may be offered. Also provides essential items such as blankets, clothing, shampoo, and deodorant. 825 NW Santiam Boulevard Mill City, OR 97360 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 897-2327 24- Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT TOD'S CORNER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, household goods (including kitchen items), bedding and linens, and other items. Accepts donations of men's clothing, kitchenware, small appliances, bedding and linens, and bathroom supplies. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 601 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with HIV who have a monthly income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: Monday 1pm-4pm, Tuesday/Thursday 2pm-5pm, Wednesday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 349-4699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER PERSONAL GOODS AND DIAPERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, hygiene items as available, and household goods, including bedding and kitchenware. Diapers are available through PDX Diaper Bank. Accepts donations of gently used clothing and household items. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Clothing and Hygiene supplies: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 771-7914 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM ASSISTANCE FOR PEOPLE WITHOUT HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry, showers, propane, tarps, and blankets. Also provides sleeping bags when available. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as homeless Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE GRESHAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides and delivers used furniture and household items. Available items vary, depending on inventory. The warehouse can typically offer sofas, dining tables, chairs, mattresses, box springs, dishes, cookware, small appliances, linens (including sheets, blankets, and pillows), and other miscellaneous household goods. 464 NE 219th Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: By referral only. Low-income individuals or families seeking essential household furnishings. Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 9am-5pm Email (503) 235-8786 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE PORTLAND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides and delivers used furniture and household items. Available items vary, depending on inventory. The warehouse can typically offer sofas, dining tables, chairs, mattresses, box springs, dishes, cookware, small appliances, linens (including sheets, blankets, and pillows), and other miscellaneous household goods. 3969 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: By referral only. Low-income individuals or families seeking essential household furnishings. Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 9am-5pm Email (503) 235-8786 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE COMMUNITY WAREHOUSE TIGARD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides and delivers used furniture and household items. Available items vary, depending on inventory. The warehouse can typically offer sofas, dining tables, chairs, mattresses, box springs, dishes, cookware, small appliances, linens (including sheets, blankets, and pillows), and other miscellaneous household goods. 9806 SW Tigard Street Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: By referral only. Low-income individuals or families seeking essential household furnishings. Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 9am-5pm Email (503) 235-8786 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DALLAS EVANGELICAL CHURCH COMMUNITY CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, towels and linens. Donations accepted. 783 SW Church Street Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9am-noon, Thursday 7pm-9pm, Friday-Saturday 9am-noon, as long as approved volunteer leaders are available Email (503) 623-3778 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH OF PRINEVILLE HOPE'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers clothing, linen, shoes, and grooming supplies. 450 SE Fairview Street Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Foster and adoptive children 0-18 years old Hours: 1st, 2nd, 3th and 5th Thursdays of the month 9am-11am; 4th Thursday 12:30pm-2:30pm Email (541) 848-0138 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, clothing, shoes, bedding, personal and feminine hygiene products, diapers, baby food and formula, assistance in acquiring identification documents, gasoline and propane vouchers, and local bus tickets. Availability is subject to donations and eligibility. 1035 SE 2nd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Food and clothing: By appointment only All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 928-4460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRANCIS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-day emergency food supply to individuals and families once every 30 days. Unhoused individuals may come once per week. Also offers used clothing, personal hygiene items, and blankets as available. Guests receiving food are served outside of the facility. Clothing is available inside the center for one guest at a time. Accepts food and clothing donations. 6535 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1:30pm Email (503) 775-6784 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FURNISH HOPE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a furniture bank of new and gently used household items, including kitchen basics such as dishes, glassware, bake ware, small appliances, utensils, pot, pans, home furniture, bedding, and cleaning supplies. 1006 SE 9th Street Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Varies by referral partner, but household must meet at least one of the following: -Individuals transitioning out of homelessness or recovery -People with disabilities or medical challenges -Refugees and people fleeing domestic violence -Foster youth and families, and aged-out foster teenagers -Veteran in the household -Families who have lost a home to a natural disaster Hours: Monday-Saturday 9am-11am (541) 638-0657 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GG'S HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides blankets, a preselected bag of food, clothing, diapers, household items, cleaning supplies, and toiletries. Other items may be available when donations are received. Accepts donations of similar items and monetary donations. 1714 Pacific Avenue Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-1pm Email (503) 359-3871 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLADSTONE PARK COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food, clothing, bedding, and linen. Families and individuals can visit the food pantry once per month. Clothing, bedding, and linen offered weekly, depending upon availability. Donations accepted. 8372 Cason Road Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Wednesday 9am-1pm Email (503) 655-4968 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS RESOURCES HELPING HANDS RESOURCES CLOTHING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, shoes, bedding, household items, and clothing for babies and newborns. Items are subject to availability. 1755 13th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday/Saturday 9am-noon, closed holidays Email (503) 364-9936 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEREFORD HOUSE FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of food. May also offer personal hygiene items, blankets, and clothing when available. 7704 N Hereford Avenue Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Friday-Saturday noon-2pm. Closed federal holidays and the Friday after Thanksgiving. Email (925) 989-9638 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HYGIENE4ALL HYGIENE4ALL HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a community hygiene, sanitation, and safety hub. Provides showers, bedding in exchange for soiled, a clothing swap, menstruation and moontime hygiene supplies, first-aid kits, bathrooms, legal disposal for household trash and needles, foot, wound, and mouth care, and a warm outdoor space with coffee and tea. Also offers paid work and opportunities for advocacy. 824 SE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Homeless individuals and families Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 3pm-8:30pm Open regardless of weather. Last shower 45 minutes before close. Search for Hygiene4all Hub on Google Maps for unanticipated holiday or other closures. Email (971) 236-2524 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK LINC MADRAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides household goods, budgeting, job, and life skills mentoring, and connections to local food banks and community meals. May also assist with rent, electric, and propane vouchers when available. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed. Utility assistance is available once per year. 278 SE 8th Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Wednesday 10am-2pm Email (541) 475-4446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH FALLS FIRST UNITED METHODIST CHURCH FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a food pantry that has non-perishable items. Blankets and sleeping bags are available to those in need, depending on donations received. Food donations such as peanut butter, canned meat, cereal, and powdered milk are accepted. 230 N 10th Street Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon Email (541) 884-4053 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LA PINE COMMUNITY KITCHEN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community meal, food pantry, clothing, blankets, personal hygiene items, and pet food. 16480 Finley Butte Road La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Meals: Monday-Friday 7am-1pm Pantry: Tuesday/Friday noon-2pm. Closed 3rd Friday of the month, open 3rd Saturday of the month 10am-noon. Clothing Closet: Tuesday/Friday 11am-2pm Email (541) 536-1312 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people with local churches and service agencies. Basic goods and services are offered to clients as they are donated. Services available may include, but are not limited to: bicycles, breakfast/lunch bags, clothing closets (adults and children), community meals, counseling services, diapers, haircuts, H.U.G.S. (hats, umbrellas, gloves, scarves, and socks), kitchen supplies, laundry service, bedding/linens, personal grooming supplies/toiletries, over-the-counter medications, personal care kits, physical therapy, showers, water bill assistance, and firewood. Accepts donations of bedding and linen, clothing, and personal grooming supplies. 200 S College Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 537-3999 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF BENTON COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, personal hygiene items, linens (bedding, towels, etc.), baby bundles (newborn baby supplies and clothing), kitchen supplies, and a gift closet (for when community members can't afford to purchase gifts for special occasions). 4900 NW Hwy 99 Suite 102 Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Low-income community members in need Hours: Helpline: Monday-Thursday 9:30am-1:30pm Email (541) 757-8111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY GAP RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides furniture, kitchen items, firewood, medical equipment, personal hygiene supplies, cleaning supplies, and bedding/linens. Accepts donations of similar items. Assistance depends on availability. Follow-ups may be provided once the need is met. Eligibility: Low-income households (self-declared or referred and verified by caseworker, employer, or landlord) Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 650-0153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF GREATER HILLSBORO GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides essential household items, including beds and bedding, linens, furniture, clothing, diapers, hygiene products, cleaning and laundry supplies, paper goods, and work boots. Donations are accepted of the items the agency distributes, with an ongoing need for couches, kitchen tables, and queen-size or smaller beds (including mattresses, box springs, and frames). Referrals to local food pantries are available. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday noon-2:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9:30am-noon Email (503) 917-0205 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF LINN COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Basic goods are offered to clients as they are donated. Goods available may include, but are not limited to household items (cleaning supplies, laundry detergent, toilet paper, and paper towels), personal care supplies (such as toothbrushes and shampoo), durable medical goods, diapers and wipes, linens, towels and blankets, kitchenware (dishes, pots and pans, glasses, etc), and baby blankets. Connects people in need with local churches and service agencies. No financial assistance is available. 1606 Santiam Road SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Low income individuals and families Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 9am-1pm, Tuesday 5pm-8pm Email (541) 924-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF TIGARD, TUALATIN, AND SHERWOOD GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, hygiene supplies, household goods, bedding/linens, over-the counter medicines and supplies, financial training, assistance with resume writing, and nutrition courses. Requests are screened and referred to outside agencies for fulfillment. Assistance is dependent upon available funding and not guaranteed. Also accepts donations of bedding/linens and personal grooming supplies. 10900 SW 121st Avenue Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9:30am-12:30pm Email (503) 443-6888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARLA'S MALL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a thrift store with books, toys, household items, new hygiene products, knick-knacks, and clothing including interview or work-appropriate attire. Offers volunteer work experience and credit recovery for students working at the store. Also accepts donations of new and gently used items. 2500 E Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Thrift Store: Unrestricted Volunteers: Baker School District high school students Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 8am-11am and 1pm-4pm, closed holidays. Hours are subject to change. Summer hours: June 16th-July 17th Monday-Thursday 8:30am-12:30pm Email (541) 524-2309 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORHOOD CENTER OF SOUTH MORROW COUNTY FOOD PANTRY AND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Food pantry provides a 3-5 day supply of food. 441 N Main Street Heppner, OR 97836 Eligibility: Households at or below 300% of the Federal Poverty Line (FPL) Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 676-5024 Get Directions More Details NEWMAN UNITED METHODIST CHURCH SINGLE PARENT MINISTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides limited, one time basic needs assistance. Assistance may include clothing, furniture, linens, towels and blankets, kitchenware (dishes, pots, pans, glasses, etc.), diapers and wipes, and personal hygiene supplies. One time limited assistance with utility payments such as telephone, water, electric, and gas. All payments are made directly to the vendor, and assistance is not guaranteed. The amount of assistance is on a case-by-case basis and current funding availability. 132 NE B Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Single parents with minor children Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-1pm Email (541) 479-5369 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION NIGHTWATCH DOWNTOWN HOSPITALITY CENTER HOSPITALITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a safe place where guests can drop in and socialize, play games, and read books. The center provides water, coffee, a hot meal, blankets, socks, and mental health services. 1432 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: People experiencing housing insecurity Hours: Thursday-Saturday 6:30pm-10pm Email (503) 220-0438 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSEBURG DREAM CENTER HOUSEHOLD GOODS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides dishes, cookware, towels, sheets, blankets, and decor when available. Also accepts donations of these items. 2555 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 10am-2pm and 4pm-7pm, Tuesday-Thursday 10am-2pm Email (541) 673-5918 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH LINCOLN RESOURCES ADVENTIST CLOTHING SHARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides gently used clothing, bedding, and towels and accepts donations of like items. 3710 Crestline Drive Waldport, OR 97394 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday 10am-2pm or by appointment Email (541) 563-3710 3 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center. Services include laundry facilities, showers, meals, phone access, voicemail services, clothing, blankets, personal care supplies, job search assistance, and information and referral to other services. Visitors can use indoor, climate-controlled seating and outdoor, shaded picnic tables. 450 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income and homeless adults without minor children Hours: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am Lunch: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday noon, Wednesday 11am Showers and laundry: 7 days per week 8:30am-noon and 2pm-5pm Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL ROSEBURG PUBLIC SHOWERS, CLOTHING CLOSET, HYGIENE SUPPLIES, BEDDING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers public showers. Also may provide a clothing closet, hygiene packages, and bedding. Donations are welcome. 630 W Stanton Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Showers: Tuesday/Thursday 3pm-5pm Clothing closet: 3rd Tuesday of the month 3:30pm-5pm Bedding, and hygiene packages: Monday/Wednesday 10am-2pm Email (541) 580-0680 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE CONTINGENT MY NEIGHBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects goods and services from community members to families and youth impacted by foster care. 809 N Russell Street #203 Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: -Resource families and foster children/youth -Biological families and relative providers with an open ODHS Child Welfare case Hours: No standard hours Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE FATHER'S HEART Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in day center with services that include two meals a day with a sack lunch to-go, showers by appointment (not wheelchair accessible), haircuts, computer and telephone access, blankets, clothing, shoes, and hygiene items. Referrals to resources surrounding housing, mental health, and substance abuse. Assistance with the determination of homelessness letters to obtain an Oregon ID. 603 12th Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Homeless adults age 18 and older and adults with families Hours: Day Center and meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm Email (503) 722-9780 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE SALVATION ARMY THE DALLES CORPS FAMILY SERVICES AND FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping style food pantry. Also provides emergency blankets, winter coats, personal grooming supplies and toiletries when available. Services are offered on a first-come, first-serve basis. 811 E 2nd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 1pm-3pm Email (541) 296-6417 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK ADVENTIST COMMUNITY SERVICES HERALD CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry and a thrift shop that offers bedding, small household items, personal hygiene supplies, and clothing. Offers community gardening opportunities. Accepts donations for the food pantry and thrift shop. 2710 First Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry and Thrift Shop: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm Donation Drop-off: Tuesday/Thursday 8:30am-2pm Email (503) 842-4794 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S FREE THRIFT STORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing and shoes including interview-ready clothes, food, home goods, kitchenware (pots, pans, dish sets), and hygiene items. Workforce closet available with work boots and shoes, rain and safety gear. Shopping allowed once per month. Exceptions made for special circumstances. Accepts donations of food, clothing, bedding, linens, kitchenware, toys and games, and hygiene supplies. 968 NW Garfield Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Thrift Store: Monday/Wednesday 1pm-3pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-noon and 1pm-3pm, 1st Saturday of the month noon-3pm -Donations: Visit website for current hours Email (541) 753-1420 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WITH LOVE RESOURCE FAMILY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides new and gently used clothing, shoes, toys, books, and supplies. Delivery is available to those living within 30 minutes of the Tigard facility. Scheduled pick-ups at the Tigard facility are available for others, Monday-Friday. There are textured hair care classes for transracial resource parents. 14555 SW 74th Avenue Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Resource parents with children in foster care ages 0-6 (up until 7th birthday) and after the child's case has changed to permanency Hours: By appointment Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details AGAPE HOUSE ESSENTIAL NEEDS AMITY CHRISTIAN CHURCH LYDIA'S CLOSET BETHLEHEM
